summary_one_line: Input must parse a well-formed XML namespace node carrying a non-NULL prefix so xmlFreeNs frees a prefixed ns

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed XML document whose tree contains a namespace declaration with a non-NULL prefix (e.g. an element like '<b k:9' introducing a prefixed name), so that xmlFreeNs is later invoked on an xmlNs whose cur->prefix != NULL and the false side of the if at tree.c:835 is taken.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile / value_profile_cmplog resolve the branch in 9-10/10 trials and hit the branch many more times (winner hitcount 15-89 vs loser 1-4); their CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs that get progressively closer to satisfying the integer/string comparisons gating XML parsing, so they assemble the prefixed-namespace tree state that drives xmlFreeNs.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog and naive block the branch in 8/10 trials with branch hitcount of only 1 and 4; their loser-blocking seeds keep mutating the leading length/header bytes (e.g. corrupted '06 00 00 00' / 'ff 7f ff ff' prefixes) and never stabilize the parse path that constructs and then frees a prefix-bearing namespace.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: CMP_MAP gradient: value_profile and value_profile_cmplog write each intercepted CMP into a distance-bucketed CMP_MAP and preserve any input that brings a comparand pair closer to matching, giving a hill-climbing gradient over the parser comparisons that naive (no CMP_MAP) and plain cmplog (CMP buffer only, no gradient feedback) lack.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The same program-side condition (a parsed namespace node with non-NULL prefix freed by xmlFreeNs), but reached by structurally recombining XML token fragments — DOCTYPE/SYSTEM/element substructure including a prefixed element like '<b k:9' — into a fresh well-formed document.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: grimoire resolves 10/10 with an enormous branch hitcount of 1959 (vs cmplog's 1); its winning seeds carry intact XML structure (' ', 'DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd"') and its lineage mixes GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator / GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator with I2S, so structural splicing of generalized token/gap runs repeatedly reconstructs prefixed-namespace trees.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (which already has I2S, the single-technique delta being grimoire's added generalization + structural mutators) blocks 8/10 with branch hitcount 1; its blocking seeds corrupt the document header bytes rather than recombining valid structural fragments, so it rarely reaches the namespace-free path.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire structural: the GeneralizationStage replaces concrete byte runs with <GAP> placeholders and the Grimoire mutators splice/recurse on these generalized token structures, repeatedly producing well-formed XML containing prefixed namespaces — a capability cmplog's havoc+I2S stack lacks.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The same parsed-namespace-with-prefix condition driving xmlFreeNs's false side, with the divergence attributable to which call-context reaches the shared parsing/free edges rather than to comparison solving.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 8/10 with branch hitcount 79 (vs naive's 4); its CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the same edges reached via the xmlTextReaderFreeNode/xmlFreeNsList call path register as new (call-context, edge) coverage and keep seeds that drive deeper into the reader's node-freeing path.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks 8/10 with branch hitcount 4; with bare edge coverage it saturates the shared edges quickly and stops rewarding inputs that reach xmlFreeNs through the distinct reader call contexts visible in the loser-blocking d=3 xmlTextReaderRead / d=2 xmlTextReaderFreeNode chain.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: context-sensitive coverage: naive_ctx's CtxHook indexes edges by call-context hash so (call-context, edge) pairs count as new coverage, preserving seeds that exercise the namespace-free path via the xmlTextReader call chain that naive's plain edge feedback treats as already-covered.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false side of the prefix-null check in xmlFreeNs, taken only when the freed namespace has a non-NULL prefix.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: Source line:     if (cur->prefix != NULL) xmlFree((char *) cur->prefix);

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked side at the branch is the false branch.
value_profile_cmplog and value_profile win their pairs via the value_profile technique delta.
value_profile beats naive with the value_profile technique delta.
The value_profile winners hit the branch far more often than their losers (15 vs 1; 89 vs 4).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=15  loser=1

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile's CMP_MAP rewards inputs whose operand pair gets closer to matching, providing a parsing gradient naive lacks.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: Loser-blocking seeds keep mutating leading length/header bytes rather than stabilizing a prefixed-namespace parse.
grimoire beats cmplog via the grimoire_structural technique delta.
grimoire resolves 10/10 with branch hitcount 1959 versus cmplog's 1.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=1959  loser=1

evidence_trail.claim: grimoire winning seeds carry intact XML structure with a prefixed element '<b k:9'.
grimoire's GeneralizationStage and structural mutators splice generalized token/gap structures, which cmplog lacks.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx beats naive via the ctx_coverage technique delta.
naive_ctx resolves 8/10 with branch hitcount 79 versus naive's 4.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=79  loser=4

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index so the same edge counts as new (call-context, edge) coverage.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is reached through the xmlTextReader free-node call chain (xmlTextReaderFreeNode at d=2).

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient (value_profile), not I2SRandReplace, so lineage I2S-floor verification is not mandatory. As corroboration I ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 6926 --role W --fuzzer grimoire --trial 1 --seed e1d7963fe300eca8`: the chain mixes GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator / GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator with I2SRandReplace and one dash row at depth 5, confirming grimoire's structural pair (hypothesis 2) but not the value_profile CMP_MAP gradient claim, which CMP_MAP feedback does not log in lineage.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose namespace node is freed via xmlFreeNs but where value_profile/naive_ctx/grimoire resolve the false side no faster than their losers (equal time-to-coverage and equal branch hitcount), showing the divergence is not driven by reaching a prefixed-namespace free.

weakest_evidence_point: The BYTE DIFF section reports no informative W-vs-L divergence, so the prefixed-namespace ('k:') feature is inferred from winner seed structure rather than a clean per-offset W/L contrast.
